raw_response: I agree with the provided explanation supporting why "Contraction is initiated by calcium binding to troponin" (Option C). However, adding another layer of detail, once calcium binds to troponin, it induces a cascade effect causing tropomyosin to move away from the myosin-binding sites on actin, thereby facilitating the power stroke required for muscle contraction.
